channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Faible] Europe revives law allowing big tech to scan for CSAM
item.description: The law known as Chat Control 2.0 passed in the European Parliament, permitting companies like Google, Meta and Microsoft to scan users' messages to hunt for CSAM.

item.title: [Critique] Ryuk ransomware member pleads guilty in the US, faces 15 years in prison
item.description: A 34-year-old Armenian man has pleaded guilty to hacking U.S. companies and deploying the infamous Ryuk ransomware to encrypt their systems. [...]

item.title: [Faible] AssuranceAmerica : 6,99 millions de clients exposés
item.description: Un important assureur confirme une fuite touchant 6,99 millions de personnes et des permis de conduire exposés.

item.title: [Critique] Ryuk operator pleads guilty; Blackcat/AlphV conspirator gets nearly 6-year sentence
item.description: One man accused of deploying Ryuk ransomware pleaded guilty Wednesday in an Oregon federal court to conspiracy and computer fraud, while another man received a 70-month federal prison sentence in a Florida court for…

item.title: [Faible] Monero tracé dans une opération menée contre le dark web
item.description: La police relie des paiements Monero à 28 suspects arrêtés dans sept pays lors d’une opération internationale contre le dark web.

item.title: [Faible] License plate cameras may be next target after Supreme Court reins in location tracking
item.description: If a warrant is ultimately needed for ALPR searches, experts say, it would radically limit how the networks of cameras can be used and would change modern policing.

item.title: [Faible] Un détenu accusé d’avoir détourné des cryptomonnaies saisies
item.description: Un détenu est accusé d’avoir blanchi 290 000 $ en cryptomonnaies déjà confisquées par la justice américaine.

item.title: [Faible] Police suspects Dutch hackers were involved in Odido breach
item.description: The Dutch National Police (Politie) says it has found "strong indications" that Dutch hackers have been involved in a February breach at the telecommunications provider Odido. [...]

item.title: [Faible] URGENT - Progress Tells ShareFile Customers to Shut Down Storage Zone Controllers Over Security Threat
item.description:
Progress Software has told ShareFile customers to shut down the Windows servers running their Storage Zone Controllers, confirming to The Hacker News that it is responding to a "credible external security threat."
The…

item.title: [Élevé] Injective Labs GitHub Compromise Pushes Wallet-Key-Stealing npm Packages
item.description: Unknown threat actors compromised the Injective Labs SDK project's GitHub repository and leveraged it to publish a malicious package on the npm registry to steal cryptocurrency wallet private keys and mnemonic seed…

item.title: [Faible] Progress urges ShareFile admins to shut down servers over “credible” threat
item.description: Progress Software is emailing ShareFile customers who use Storage Zone Controllers to immediately shut down their servers after identifying what it describes as a "credible external security threat" targeting the…

item.title: [Faible] Six New U-Boot Flaws Could Let Malicious Images Crash Devices or Run Code at Boot
item.description: Researchers at firmware security firm Binarly have found six new flaws in U-Boot, the small program that starts up hardware as varied as home routers, smart cameras, and the management chips inside data-center…

item.title: [Critique] Hackers exploit critical auth bypass in Gitea Docker image
item.description: Hackers are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in the official Docker image for the Gitea self-hosted Git service that allows attackers to impersonate any user, including administrators. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Money launderer accused of stealing seized crypto while in prison
item.description: A Bulgarian national has been charged with stealing $290,000 in government-seized cryptocurrency while serving 121 months in prison for helping launder millions stolen from American fraud victims. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] Laser Attack Resets Tangem Wallet Passwords on Cards That Can't Be Patched
item.description: Researchers at Ledger's Donjon security team have shown that a precisely timed laser pulse, aimed at the chip inside a Tangem crypto wallet card, can reset the card's password to anything the attacker…

item.title: [Moyen] Researcher Details WhatsApp-to-Host Attack Chain Using Three OpenClaw Flaws
item.description: Details have emerged about three now-patched security flaws in the OpenClaw personal artificial intelligence (AI) assistant that, if successfully exploited, could enable credential theft, privilege escalation, and…

item.title: [Faible] The Replicant in Your Directory: AI Agents and the Identity Security Gap
item.description: AI agents are accelerating the growth of non-human identities, making it harder for organizations to understand what exists, who owns it, and what it can access. Netwrix explains why stronger visibility and identity…

item.title: [Critique] China, India ran separate spying campaigns against same Pakistani police force
item.description: The activity, in some cases breaching the exact same systems, ran between February 2024 and April 2026 and centered on the force responsible for the country’s southwestern province that has been the site of a…

item.title: [Faible] New MODBEACON RAT Uses gRPC Streaming for Encrypted C2 Traffic
item.description:
The China-linked cybercrime group known as Silver Fox has been attributed to a new Rust-based remote access trojan (RAR) called MODBEACON.
Chinese cybersecurity company QiAnXin said that while the threat cluster may…

item.title: [Moyen] Unpatched XRING Flaw in XQUIC Lets Remote Clients Crash HTTP/3 Servers
item.description:
A single wrong variable on one line in XQUIC, Alibaba's QUIC and HTTP/3 library, lets any remote client crash the server with a short burst of completely legal traffic. There is no patch.
FoxIO researcher Sébastien…

item.title: [Critique] Zimbra urges customers to patch critical web client XSS flaw
item.description: The Zimbra security team urged customers to patch a critical vulnerability affecting the Classic Web Client used to access the Zimbra Collaboration suite. [...]

item.title: [Faible] From 17,000 to 1.1 Million Assets: How Lumen Technologies Rebuilt Exposure Management at Scale
item.description: Most enterprises assume their asset inventory is close enough to accurate. The evidence suggests otherwise. According to a survey of over 600 security leaders in the 2026 Axonius Actionability Report, only 45% of…

item.title: [Faible] Exposed Hacker Server Reveals WP-SHELLSTORM Backdooring Thousands of WordPress Sites
item.description: A cybercrime crew left one of its own servers wide open on the internet for three weeks, and it exposed the operation's inner workings: the hacking tools, the activity logs, and target lists naming more than 1.4 million…

item.title: [Faible] Study of 281 Free Android VPN Apps Finds Traffic Leaks, Unencrypted Data, and Tracking
item.description: Researchers ran 281 of the most popular free VPN apps on the Google Play Store through a new testing system and found that many fail at the basics people install a VPN for, i.e., keeping their traffic private and…

item.title: [Faible] Hackers Use Fake Microsoft Entra Passkey Enrollment to Gain Microsoft 365 Access
item.description: A threat actor has been targeting organizations spanning multiple sectors with voice-based fake security requests that prompt Microsoft 365 users to enroll a new Entra passkey with an aim to carry out data extortion…

item.title: [Moyen] Attackers Exploit 'Ill Bloom' Vulnerability to Drain $3.1 Million From Cryptocurrency Wallets
item.description: Security firm Coinspect has disclosed a crypto wallet flaw it calls Ill Bloom, and attackers are already using it. The flaw is in how some wallet software generated its recovery phrase, the words that…

item.title: [Critique] Former ransomware negotiator gets 4 years for BlackCat attacks
item.description: A former employee of cybersecurity incident response company DigitalMint was sentenced to 70 months in prison for targeting U.S. companies in BlackCat (ALPHV) ransomware attacks. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Ransomware Negotiator Gets 70 Months in Prison for Aiding BlackCat Attacks
item.description: A 41-year-old former ransomware negotiator has been sentenced to nearly six years (i.e., 70 months) in prison in the U.S. for their role in conspiring with the now-defunct BlackCat ransomware operators to extort…

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-48939 — iCagenda Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type Vulnerability
item.description: iCagenda contains an unrestricted upload of file with dangerous type vulnerability that allows the upload of arbitrary files in the file attachment feature, ultimately resulting in PHP code upload and execution.

item.title: [Critique] CVE-2026-56291 — Balbooa Forms Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type Vulnerability
item.description: Balbooa Forms contains an unrestricted upload of file with dangerous type vulnerability that allows an unauthenticated arbitrary file upload which could allow uploading of executable files leading to full RCE.

item.title: [Faible] Dutch police trace Odido telco cyberattack to suspected local accomplice
item.description: Dutch police said Thursday they had uncovered evidence suggesting that Dutch criminals were involved in the cyberattack on telecom provider Odido that exposed the personal data of more than 6 million customers earlier…

item.title: [Faible] OpenMandriva Linux says contributor tried to sabotage the project
item.description: The OpenMandriva Linux project announced that it was the target of an attempted act of internal sabotage after a dispute among contributors. [...]
